meetingTitle: Discuss AI Advisor-Coach role for Churchill/30PlusStrong
lineageTag: meeting:Discuss AI Advisor-Coach role for Churchill/30Plus|date:2026-04-16

actions.title: Reconnect with Jane to continue AI Advisor-Coach discussion
actions.context:
**Background**: Greg connected Cian with Jane from Churchill / 30PlusStrong to explore a potential **AI Advisor-Coach** role. Churchill is a global executive coaching firm (founded by Jane 14 years ago) that was acquired last year by 30PlusStrong, a California-based organization with a government focus. Jane is staying on as CEO for two more years part-time.

**Discussion**: Jane explained that Churchill works with directors through C-suite at large corporations, providing executive coaching and leadership development. They've observed that clients increasingly ask for **advice** alongside coaching — especially around AI — and are launching a new "Advisor-Coach" program. This program pairs experienced ex-executive coaches with advisory capabilities so they can fluidly move between coaching and advising. Jane said: *"We were looking for someone that would be a great advisor coach in the AI world. And Greg suggested we have a conversation."* Cian shared that he wasn't sure whether the AI need was internal (transforming Churchill's own practices) or client-facing. Jane began to clarify that the new boss at 30PlusStrong is also *"looking at vendors to help us with AI as a company,"* suggesting there may be both an internal and external opportunity.

**Decision/Direction**: No decision was reached. The call was cut short due to severe audio issues — Jane was at a Dunkin' Donuts with background music interfering. Cian sent his phone number via chat so they could reconnect.

**Next steps**: Cian needs to follow up with Jane (or wait for her callback) to complete the introductory conversation and learn more about the scope of the AI Advisor-Coach role, whether it's client-facing advisory, internal AI transformation for 30PlusStrong, or both.
